content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule. Looking at atom O:13 (oxygen with index 13), I can see it's connected to:

- C:11 (carbon atom with index 11) - shown by the yellow/gold bond
- H:14 (hydrogen atom with index 14) - shown by the white/gray bond

The image clearly shows O:13 has exactly two covalent bonds: one to carbon (C:11) and one to hydrogen (H:14). This forms a hydroxyl group (-OH).

From the file naming in the working directory, I can confirm that the geomconf_index is 4132 (from the filename "geomconf_4132.xyz").

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom O:13 are:
- Atom index 11, element C (carbon)
- Atom index 14, element H (hydrogen)

And the geomconf_index for this molecule is 4132.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 13, whose element should be O. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
